title: Welcome to Phandalin

location.name: Phandalin
location.description: A small frontier town built on the ruins of a much older settlement. Phandalin is a rough-and-tumble place — simple log buildings, muddy roads, and a handful of shops and businesses serving the miners, farmers, and travelers who pass through.

read_aloud: You arrive in Phandalin — a small, rough frontier town of about fifty simple log buildings. Muddy wagon tracks crisscross the main street, and a few townsfolk eye your group with a mix of curiosity and hope. The town has clearly seen better days, but there's life here. Smoke rises from chimneys, and the sound of hammers rings from the smithy. A sign near the center of town reads 'Barthen's Provisions.'

encounters.description: Meeting the various NPCs around town. Each has information or quests to offer.

npcs.name: Elmar Barthen
dialog_hooks: Pays the party for delivering the wagon (10 gp each as Gundren promised)
Gundren hasn't arrived yet — seems worried
Warns about the Redbrands — a gang of ruffians led by someone called 'Glasstaff' who have been shaking down locals

npcs.name: Toblen Stonehill
dialog_hooks: Runs the Stonehill Inn — rooms for 5 sp/night
The Redbrands hassle every business in town
Thel Dendrar, a local woodcarver, stood up to the Redbrands. They murdered him, took his wife and children
The Redbrands hang out at the Sleeping Giant tap house on the east end of town

npcs.name: Linene Graywind
dialog_hooks: Runs the Lionshield Coster trading post
Recognizes the stolen supplies from Cragmaw Hideout — pays 50 gp reward if returned
Refuses to sell weapons to the Redbrands

npcs.name: Halia Thornton
dialog_hooks: Runs the Phandalin Miner's Exchange
Secretly a Zhentarim agent — ambitious and cunning

quest.name: Eliminate Glasstaff
quest.objective: Kill the Redbrand leader Glasstaff and bring her any correspondence found in his quarters

npcs.name: Harbin Wester
dialog_hooks: Townsmaster — cowardly, avoids confrontation with the Redbrands
Posts bounties on the notice board

quest.name: Orc Trouble
quest.objective: A band of orcs has set up camp near Wyvern Tor. Travelers have been attacked. Clear them out.

npcs.name: Qelline Alderleaf
dialog_hooks: Halfling farmer, wise and kind
Her son Carp saw Redbrands emerge from a secret tunnel in the woods south of Tresendar Manor
Suggests the party visit Reidoth the Druid in Thundertree — he knows every inch of the land and could help find Cragmaw Castle and Wave Echo Cave

quest.name: Find Reidoth the Druid
quest.objective: Reidoth the Druid in the ruins of Thundertree knows the location of Cragmaw Castle and Wave Echo Cave

npcs.name: Sister Garaele
dialog_hooks: Elf cleric of Tymora at the Shrine of Luck
Recently returned from a failed diplomatic mission to Agatha's Lair — she looks exhausted

quest.name: The Banshee's Bargain
quest.objective: Bring a jeweled silver comb to the banshee Agatha in exchange for answering one question: where is Bowgentle's spellbook?

npcs.name: Daran Edermath
dialog_hooks: Retired half-elf adventurer, Order of the Gauntlet member
Troubled by undead activity near Old Owl Well

quest.name: Old Owl Well
quest.objective: Investigate reports of undead near the Old Owl Well ruins east of town
quest.reward: Daran's gratitude and Order of the Gauntlet reputation

npcs.name: Mirna Dendrar
dialog_hooks: Found as a prisoner in the Redbrand hideout with her two children
Her husband Thel was murdered by the Redbrands
Offers information about a family heirloom emerald necklace hidden in the herb shop in Thundertree as reward for rescue

loot.location: Barthen pays 10 gp each for 6 party members for wagon delivery

skill_checks.success: Halia seems to have her own agenda — she's more interested in Glasstaff's papers than stopping the Redbrands.
skill_checks.failure: Halia seems like a concerned businesswoman.

dm_notes: This is a sandbox act — let the party explore town at their own pace. Present NPCs as they visit locations. The Redbrand threat should become clear through multiple NPC conversations. Don't rush them to the hideout. The side quests set up Chapter 3 but shouldn't be pursued yet — they're hooks to remember. Eventually the Redbrand confrontation (Act II) should feel inevitable.
